name: Emil Richards
abstract: Emil Richards (born Emilio Joseph Radocchia; September 2, 1932 – December 13, 2019) was an American vibraphonist and percussionist.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: The other guy on it was Emil Richards, playing vibes, who is also another great drummer. ... Emil Richards went to India to study and also buy instruments for his work as a motion-picture percussionist. I respect that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Now, you mentioned something about you and… it could have been… I just found it was either Joe Roland or it was Johnnie Ray or Emil Richards. [INTERVIEWEE] It was… in those days it was Johnnie Ray.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I basically just walked in and played the charts that were put in front of me... Emil Richards on percussion
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny Ray, this other vibraphone player with Shearing, and Emil did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012
